Why Playfair

Belonging isn't a nice-to-have. It's a retention strategy.

Early friendships are one of the strongest predictors of whether a student stays. Plenty of universities now book Playfair every year, not as a one-off bit of fun, but as a real part of how they keep students enrolled.

The number that gets budgets approved

Retention climbed 1–11% after a single event.

A study across 32 universities found one Playfair event lifted retention by 1 to 11%. That's worth anywhere from $121,000 at smaller schools to over $2.6 million at larger ones, in a single year.

The big idea

Students rarely leave because of the coursework. They leave because they never found their people.

Every student walking onto your campus is quietly asking the same thing. Will I find my group? Will anyone here know my name by next week? Here is why a single Playfair event moves the number universities care about most.

Read Full StoryDownload our brochure ›
The full story

Why belonging keeps students enrolled.

The first weeks of university decide more than any prospectus. Here is what the research says, and what Playfair does about it.

Ask any student why they stayed, and they rarely lead with the lectures. They talk about the people. The friend who saved them a seat, the group chat that formed in the first week, the sense that someone would notice if they didn't show up. Belonging is quiet, but it is the thing that turns an enrolment into a graduate.

The first few weeks set the trajectory. A student who has made real friends by the end of week one walks into the rest of the year with a reason to come back. A student who hasn't is doing the hardest thing in higher education completely alone, and they are the ones most likely to quietly disappear before census date.

What the research shows

This isn't a hunch. A study across 32 universities found that a single Playfair event lifted first-year retention by between 1 and 11 per cent. Put in budget terms, that is worth anywhere from around $121,000 at smaller schools to over $2.6 million at larger ones, in a single year.

The reason it works is structural, not lucky. Playfair is engineered to manufacture dozens of small, low-stakes interactions in a short window, so that by the time students leave the room they have already met far more people than they would in a typical first month.

Why Playfair, specifically

Every activity is inclusive and never a contest, so introverts, international students and first-in-family students feel just as welcome as anyone. Senior students are trained as peer leaders and woven into the show, so new students immediately see that this is a place where people look out for each other.

That is why universities stop treating Playfair as entertainment and start treating it as infrastructure, the same way they would a tutoring centre or a wellbeing service. It is one of the cheapest, fastest levers a campus has to move the number that matters most.

Talk to us ›Back to home
The main event · On campus

Ninety minutes. A hall full of strangers. A year group that clicks.

This is the heart of what we do. Hundreds of students on their feet, moving through fast, playful activities that open up into quieter one-on-one moments, then build right back up again. It's not a lecture, not a mixer, not a stand-around welcome.

What it actually looks like

Loud, warm, and impossible to sit out.

From the first minute the whole room is up and moving. There is no sign-up sheet, no awkward circle, no waiting to be picked. A facilitator runs the room like a conductor, and the energy does the introductions for you.

Then it dips into quieter moments, two people sharing the story behind their name, before lifting right back into the big-room buzz. By the end, students have spoken to dozens of people they would never have met.

First few minutes

Big-room energisers

Playful, whole-room activities that take the pressure out of meeting someone new. Everyone's in from the first minute.

Warming up

Rotating partners

Students keep swapping partners on purpose, so they meet far beyond the handful of people they already know.

Halfway in

Small-group moments

The story behind your name. What you have in common. The little conversations that turn acquaintances into friends.

Throughout

Your seniors, leading

We train your senior students as peer leaders and weave them into the show, so new students see this is a place people look out for each other.

Start to finish

Built for everyone

Every activity is inclusive and never a contest. Introverts, international students, and first-in-family students feel just as welcome as anyone.

After the event

Friendships that stick

The groups that form in the first ninety minutes are the ones students keep, long after orientation week is over.

Good to know

What we need from you.

Almost nothing. Playfair is built to drop into orientation week with very little setup on your side.

A big space

An open hall, gym, quad or field, anywhere a few hundred students can stand and move freely.

About 90 minutes

Slot us into your schedule, ideally day one. We bring the structure, the host and the energy.

A few seniors

Point us to some returning students and we will train them as peer leaders before the day.

Bring Playfair to your campus ›
Also available · Online

When they can't all be in the room.

On campus is the main event. But some students can't be there in person, especially international and off-campus students. Playfair Online brings them the same energy through the screen, so nobody starts the year on the outside.

  • The same upbeat, community-building energy, live
  • Whole-class moments plus fast breakout rooms
  • Runs on the video tools you already use
  • Made for students in any time zone
How it runs

Big room, small rooms, repeat.

A live host keeps the whole cohort together, then sends students into fast, friendly breakouts and pulls them straight back, again and again.

One big welcome

Everyone starts together in a single, high-energy room, hosted live by a real facilitator.

Fast breakouts

We drop students into quick small-group rooms to actually talk, then bring them back to the buzz.

Any device, anywhere

It runs on the tools you already use, for students in any time zone, on a laptop or a phone.

Ask about Playfair Online ›
Beyond campus · Corporate

The same magic, for teams and conferences.

The thing that warms up a room of first-years works just as well on a room of colleagues. Conference kickoffs, team days, graduate inductions, we tune Playfair for adults without losing any of the energy.

Conference kickoffs

Open your event with real connection instead of awkward name-tag small talk.

Team days & offsites

Get people genuinely talking, the kind of thing that carries back into the office.

Graduate inductions

Help new starters feel like part of the team from their very first week.

Why it works for grown-ups

The awkward first ten minutes, gone.

Adults are just first-years in better shoes. Put strangers in a room and the same hesitation shows up, the polite nods, the phones, the huddle of people who already know each other. Playfair dissolves it fast, on purpose, with structure that makes connection feel effortless rather than forced.

We scale the energy to the room, from a ten-person leadership offsite to a thousand-strong conference floor, and we tune the content so it lands with professionals without ever feeling like a trust-fall seminar.
